choiceresponse.label:
Consider two patients who differ only in vessel-related indicators:
Patient C: ca_0 = 1
Patient D: ca_2 = 1Which statements are true about the model’s predictions? (Select all that apply.)

checkboxgroup.choice: The model predicts higher heart disease risk for Patient C than for Patient D.
The model predicts higher heart disease risk for Patient D than for Patient C.
The difference comes from the relative signs and magnitudes of the ca_0 and ca_2 coefficients.
This implies that having zero vessels is clinically worse than having two vessels.
The model treats these indicators as separate additive contributions.

div.p: The model assigns a positive contribution when ca_0 = 1 and a negative contribution when ca_2 = 1. The predicted difference follows directly from these signs and magnitudes. These are separate additive indicator variables in a linear predictor. Each one contributes its own coefficient to the log-odds, and the model just sums them. These statements describe how the model behaves, not real-world clinical effects.
